assertions.description: The fc980c is a capacitive keyboard controller for the Topre FC980C. The key matrix uses an external matrix-scanning IC connected to the keyboard via a 20-pin 'KEYBOARD' JST connector (J3). Row and column selection is communicated via 3-bit ROW_BIT[0:2] and 4-bit COL_BIT[0:3] encoded addresses, plus KEY_STATE and KEY_ENABLE control signals. The analyzer's key_matrices detector finds nothing because it looks for conventional direct ROW/COL nets with diodes. This is a false negative — the design definitely contains keyboard matrix logic, just at a higher abstraction level. A note in design_observations or a pattern matching 'bit-encoded keyboard matrix' signals would be valuable.
